digest.top_line: BART: adjuvant pelvic RT post-cystectomy cuts 2y locoregional recurrence to 8% vs 26% (LRFFS HR 0.43, p=0.04); OS unchanged (HR 0.78).
digest.tldr: Bladder carried the day: BART is the first RCT of adjuvant pelvic RT after radical cystectomy, roughly halving locoregional recurrence (8% vs 26%, p=0.006) in high-risk MIBC. The LRFFS surrogate hit but OS was null (HR 0.78, p=0.31), and no pt got adjuvant nivolumab, leaving applicability to current IO pathways open.

sites.intro: First randomised evidence for adjuvant pelvic RT post-cystectomy in high-risk MIBC, a locoregional-control win decided before the adjuvant-IO era.

studies.name: BART
studies.tldr: 2y locoregional FFS 87.1% vs 76.0% (HR 0.43, p=0.04) with adjuvant pelvic RT post-cystectomy; OS not significant (HR 0.78, p=0.31).

details.text: 🔍 Phase III multicentre RCT, N=153, high-risk MIBC post-radical cystectomy + chemo, randomised to adjuvant RT vs observation

details: 🔍 RT: 50.4Gy/28fx to cystectomy bed + pelvic nodes (common/int/ext iliac, presacral, obturator), stoma/bowel-sparing IMRT, daily IGRT
📊 Locoregional recurrence 8% (RT) vs 26% (obs), p=0.006; no isolated locoregional recurrences in the RT arm

details.text: 📊 2y LRFFS benefit by subgroup (HR vs observation)

details: 🔗 Historic locoregional recurrence ~30% post-cystectomy in high-risk pts (≥pT3, N+, <10 nodes, LVI+, margin+); chemo little help locally
⚠️ OS not significant (HR 0.78, p=0.31); the 1° endpoint was a locoregional surrogate, not OS

details.text: ⚠️ 14/77 RT-arm pts never received RT (refused/progressed/infeasible), analysed as observation; ITT HR 0.43 vs per-protocol HR 0.27

details: ⚠️ No immunotherapy given; adjuvant nivolumab (CheckMate-274) now standard, limiting applicability to current pathways

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase III, prespecified 2y LRFFS primary hit, diverging from the current no-adjuvant-RT norm. Underpowered and OS not significant, so short of practice-changing.
verdict.audience: High-risk (T3-4/N+/R+) MIBC, post-cystectomy + chemo, no immunotherapy

studies.significance: The LRFFS benefit concentrates in the pN+ and T3+ subgroups (2y HR 0.22 and 0.25), and per-protocol it widens to HR 0.27 (93.2% vs 75.0%) once the 14 who never received RT are analysed as observation. Standard 50.4Gy/28fx to bed plus pelvic nodes transfers directly, so this moves the adjuvant-pelvic-RT decision for node-positive or margin-positive disease.
studies.monday_clinic: In pN+ or margin-positive high-risk MIBC after cystectomy and cisplatin chemo, this supports weighing adjuvant pelvic RT for locoregional control; it does not establish an OS benefit and does not extend to low-risk node-negative, margin-negative disease.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 multicentre RCT, 1:1, **N=153** (RT 77 / obs 76), enrolled 2016-2024, stratified by nodal stage (N0/N+) and chemotherapy. Median follow-up **47 mo**. **Underpowered**: accrual fell short of the sample-size goal.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **High-risk (T3-4, N1-3, or R+)** non-metastatic urothelial MIBC after radical cystectomy. 62% pT3-4, 41% pN+, 28% variant-histology component; median age 57, median 20 nodes dissected, 4.6% positive margins, 2.6% neobladder.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **50.4 Gy / 28 fx** to cystectomy bed plus pelvic nodes (common / internal / external iliac, presacral, obturator). Stoma- and bowel-sparing IMRT with daily onboard image guidance.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** 2-year locoregional failure-free survival. Secondary: bladder-cancer-specific survival, DFS, overall survival. Fine-Gray competing-risk analysis (distant mets, non-cancer death).

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary **met**; the time-to-event secondaries (DFS, BCSS, OS) all favoured RT numerically but **none reached significance**.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Late grade 3+ toxicity comparable between arms; acute grade 2 GI higher with RT while grade 3 GI was lower, with **no toxicity-related discontinuation**.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Adjuvant RT after cystectomy is not routine (historic locoregional recurrence ~30% in high-risk pts); BART is the **largest RCT** in this space. A planned **MERCY** individual-patient-data meta-analysis will test the OS question.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk (T3-4, N1-3, or R+) urothelial MIBC after radical cystectomy and cisplatin-based chemo, without immunotherapy. Does not represent low-risk (≤pT2 N0 R0) disease and predates routine adjuvant nivolumab.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Underpowered, **OS not significant (HR 0.78, p=0.31)** on a locoregional surrogate primary. 14/77 RT-arm pts never received RT, so the **ITT HR (0.43)** understates the per-protocol effect (**HR 0.27**). No immunotherapy used.

open_questions: OS benefit of adjuvant RT (planned MERCY IPD meta-analysis)
Adjuvant RT plus immunotherapy after cystectomy

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Radiotherapy After Cystectomy for Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: A radical cystectomy + extended pelvic lymph node dissection is considered to be the treatment of choice for patients with muscle invasive bladder cancer (MIBC). Despite this aggressive treatment the outcome is poor and ultimately, 30% of the patients with ≥pT3 tumors develop a pelvic recurrence. One- and 2-years survival for patients developing a local recurrence after cystectomy is only 8% and 3% respectively, with a median survival of \<4 months. For patients with lymph node recurrence…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria: muscle invasive bladder cancer with:

* ≥ pathological tumor stage (p)T3 stage + presence of lymphovascular invasion on pathological examination
* pT4
* \<10 lymph nodes removed
* positive lymph nodes
* positive surgical margins

Exclusion Criteria:

\-

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Radiotherapy in Patients With Pathological High-risk Bladder Cancer (GETUG-AFU 30)
related_trials.brief_summary:
This is a randomized multicentre study in patients with high-risk MIBC to investigate adjuvant radiotherapy after radical cystectomy and pelvic lymph node dissection.

The objective of the study is to provide evidence that adjuvant radiotherapy improves loco-regional control with potential benefits in survival. The study will also evaluate the quality of life of patients and the tolerance of the treatment.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:
